summary: Obfuscated bulk email/SMS spam and phishing toolkit
details: nolimit-x@1.0.236 is an offensive bulk-messaging and credential-phishing framework distributed via npm. The package self-describes as an 'Advanced email sender' with keywords including 'red-team' and 'smtp', and ships ~600KB of heavily obfuscated JavaScript under a hidden.ad/ directory (main entry.ad/x0.js plus 30+ x*.js modules, sms-providers.js, phone-validator.js, smtp-health-cache.js, web-command.js, from-generator.js). The obfuscated code is packed with obfuscator.io string-array rotation, an RC4+base64 string decoder, self-defending function wrappers, and debug-protection counters — a build step (scripts.build → node scripts/obfuscate.js) and a devDependency on javascript-obfuscator confirm intentional concealment. The CLI exposes subcommands ('auth', 'extract', 'web') implementing bulk SMTP sending, SMS dispatch via Twilio/Plivo/MessageBird, Microsoft device-code OAuth phishing flows, IMAP/Microsoft Graph contact harvesting, MX-based recipient sorting, DKIM key generation, an open-redirect scanner, and a Chrome-extension 'WebSend' panel that injects a sending UI into the victim's open webmail session. The package's purpose is to attack third parties (mass-spam recipients, OAuth phishing targets, webmail account holders); installing it provides ready-made abuse infrastructure. Although the import-time / install-time path does not itself exfiltrate the operator's secrets, the package as published is functionally malware aimed at outside victims and violates npm acceptable-use policy.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
